memoryArtifacts.headline: How Margery and Owen met
memoryArtifacts.description: Story passed down the family, my Dad told this to me: Margery Browne and Owen O'Sullivan. Owen were on the same ship crossing, he was much older. She was a stowaway. He protected her. She was a remark …

Sullivan Family Burial Ground
Durham, Strafford County, New Hampshire, USA 
MEMORIAL ID	66020240

popularStory.headline: How Margery and Owen met
popularStory.description: Story passed down the family, my Dad told this to me: Margery Browne and Owen O'Sullivan. Owen were on the same ship crossing, he was much older. She was a stowaway. He protected her. She was a remark …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret or Margery Browne於1714年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克時，她的父親Patrick Browne34歲，母親Mrs. Browne30歲。她於大約1735年與John Owen Sullivan在美國新罕布夏州結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1801年在美國緬因州約克貝維其可享年87歲，埋葬在美國新罕布夏州斯特拉福德達勒姆沙利文 派利 布利亞爾 格洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret or Margery Browne于1714年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克时，她的父亲Patrick Browne34岁，母亲Mrs. Browne30岁。她于大约1735年与John Owen Sullivan在美国新罕布什尔州结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1801年在美国缅因州約克貝維其可享年87岁，埋葬在美国新罕布什尔州斯特拉福德達勒姆沙利文 派利 布利亞爾 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

historicEvents.title: Second State to Ratify U.S. Constitution
historicEvents.description: On December 12, 1787, Pennsylvania ratified the U.S. Constitution.

historicEvents.title: New Hampshire Helps Ratify the US Constitution
historicEvents.description: On June 21, 1788, New Hampshire became the ninth and final state needed to ratify the US Constitution and make it the official law of the land

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret or Margery Browne was born in 1714, in Cork, County Cork, Ireland, her father, Patrick Browne, was 34 and her mother, Mrs. Browne, was 30. She married John Owen Sullivan about 1735, in New Hampshire, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1801, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 87, and was buried in Sullivan Family Burial Ground, Durham, Strafford, New Hampshire, United States.

Sie hat um 1735 in New Hampshire, Vereinigte Staaten John Owen Sullivan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. ja.lifeSummary: Margaret or Margery Browneが1714年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクコルクで生まれた時，父Patrick Browneは34歳，母Mrs. Browneは30歳であった。約1735年にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州でJohn Owen Sullivanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1801年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックで87歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ストラフォードダルハムスリヴァン ファミリー ブリアル グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.Sullivan Family Burial Ground, Durham, S: 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 뒤람 설리반 파밀리 버이앨 그라운드
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret or Margery Browne(이)가 1714년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Patrick Browne(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Browne(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1735년 미국 뉴햄프셔 주에서 John Owen Sullivan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1801년 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 스트래퍼드 뒤람 설리반 파밀리 버이앨 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret or Margery Browne nasceu em 1714, em Cork, County Cork, Irlanda, seu pai, Patrick Browne, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Browne, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John Owen Sullivan aproximadamente 1735, em Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1801, em Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 87 anos, e foi sepultada em Sullivan Family Burial Ground, Durham, Strafford, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Sullivan Family Burial Ground, Durham, S: сулливан фамили буряль гроунд, дурам, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret or Margery Browne родилась в 1714 г. в местности корк, коунти корк, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Patrick Browne было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Browne, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Owen Sullivan около 1735 г. в местности Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1801 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 87 лет и была похоронена в местности сулливан фамили буряль гроунд, дурам, страффорд, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret or Margery Browne nació en 1714, en Cork, County Cork, Irlanda, su papá, Patrick Browne, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Mrs. Browne, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John Owen Sullivan aproximadamente 1735, en Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1801, en Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultada en Sullivan Family Burial Ground, Durham, Strafford, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos.
